mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL indicates persistent inflation that erodes real earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations following month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases, or the Rank IC between CPIAUCSL changes and 20-day returns is positive over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, further increases in the policy rate tighten financial conditions abruptly, increasing corporate borrowing costs and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the mean return during triggered periods is not significantly lower than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales signal a contraction in both labor income and consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 1-month change negative and RSAFS 1-month change negative
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when both PAYEMS and RSAFS decline month-over-month, or a regression interaction term between the two series changes is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) with the wrong sign.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a declining unemployment rate signals an overheating economy that prompts additional hawkish policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During tightening cycles, months with declining UNRATE are followed by positive 5-day S&P returns in more than 55% of cases, or the spread between returns on declining-UNRATE versus rising-UNRATE months is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production signals expanding corporate output and revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO increases are followed by negative 20-day S&P returns in more than 50% of observations, or the Rank IC between INDPRO changes and 20-day returns is negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly level of housing starts below 1,350 thousand units signals deficient construction activity and weakening residential investment, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when HOUST is below 1,350, or the mean 60-day return during triggered periods is not significantly lower than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS produce stagflationary pressure where rising input costs coincide with tightening liquidity, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change positive and FEDFUNDS 3-month change positive
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of positive 3-month changes in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to produce negative 5-day returns (positive in more than 55% of joint-trigger instances), or the regression interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) with the wrong sign.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating pace of retail sales growth signals robust consumer demand that lifts corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month RSAFS increases are followed by negative 20-day S&P returns in more than 50% of observations, or the Rank IC between RSAFS changes and 20-day returns is negative over any 2-year rolling window.
